persona_05_blocker_table.blocked_reason: weak-source dominated or insufficiently cross-source robust | thin evidence | workbook policy constraint: top_3_cluster_share_of_core_labeled | weak_source_cost_centers_present
persona_05_blocker_table.deck_ready_claim_reason: Blocked or constrained persona remains ineligible for deck-ready claim wording.

notable_findings: Only one selected representative example is available for persona_05, which is too thin for a stronger deck-ready claim layer.
Many top-fit rows are vendor or platform troubleshooting threads that inherit tool_limitation_workaround signals but do not read like a stable cross-tool persona narrative.
The strongest legitimate persona_05 rows usually describe manual reporting blocked by product limits while trying to produce presentation-ready output under time pressure.
A large share of boundary rows read like a tail of persona_01 manual-reporting burden or persona_03 tool-limitation workaround rather than a clearly separate fifth persona.

persona_05_vs_persona_01_04.true_fifth_persona_or_tail: under-evidenced hybrid tail between persona_01 and persona_03, not yet a clean fifth anchor

comparison_notes.persona_01: High overlap. persona_05 shares manual_reporting as primary bottleneck and often differs only by adding tool_limitation_workaround and deadline/presentation pressure. This looks more like a constrained sub-tail than a clean new anchor.
comparison_notes.persona_02: Moderate overlap. persona_02 centers on root-cause-analysis difficulty and numbers-visible-but-not-explainable, while persona_05 is more execution/tool-limit heavy.
comparison_notes.persona_03: High overlap. persona_03 is the cleaner pure tool_limitation_workaround persona. persona_05 often reads like manual-reporting plus persona_03-style tool limits.
comparison_notes.persona_04: Low overlap. persona_04 is about reconciliation, mistrust, and repeated validation before sending, which is more distinct from persona_05.

selected_examples.why_selected: Selected for grounding fit, bottleneck specificity, persona fit, defining axis match. Matches persona on bottleneck_type=tool_limitation, user_role=analyst, tool_dependency_mode=bi_dashboard_heavy

claim_eligibility_simulation.interpretation: Current state preserves strictness and avoids overclaiming a thin fifth persona.
This would pass source-tier checks but would bypass thin-evidence and distinctiveness safeguards.
persona_05 fails immediately because selected_example_count=1 and evidence_confidence_tier=thin.
This adds process ambiguity without fixing the real evidence problem.
Safest current choice until evidence quality improves.

exact_blocker: persona_05 is core-anchored but still fails the thin-evidence and representative-example sufficiency bar, and its narrative boundary with persona_01 and persona_03 is too blurry for safe fifth-persona claim wording.
recommended_backlog_detail: Increase representative-example depth before any claim reconsideration; one selected example is not enough.
Audit and prune source-specific support threads currently inheriting tool_limitation_workaround into persona_05.
Re-test semantic distinctiveness specifically against persona_01 manual-reporting burden and persona_03 tool-limitation workaround after better example grounding is available.
